title: Chapter 3 – Extermination of the Family (2)
content:
'What was that?'

He dropped his gaze again. Her look had been unreadable, but he chose not to dwell on it.

'It's not as though the Zhongnan Sect masters are going to demonstrate their martial arts right here in the courtyard.'

Seeing them in person was enough. He could feel their energy from where he stood.

The spiritual presence of Mount Zhongnan, the cradle of Taoism, was unmistakable. It was nothing like the qi of the Jeong family's martial artists, steeped as they were in worldly concerns.

A bracing clarity washed over his skin. He could only wonder how deep their internal cultivation ran to produce such an aura.

"Raise your heads. We didn't come here to admire the tops of your skulls."

Yeo Il-sin spoke, laughter still lingering in her voice. One by one, the household members straightened their postures.

Clan Leader Jeong Dae-myeong hastily wiped the cold sweat from his brow and stepped forward.

"Forgive our discourtesy in leaving the esteemed Zhongnan swordsmen standing. Please, allow me to guide you inside."

The Zhongnan Sect masters fell into step behind him.

As Yeo Il-sin passed the assembled family members, her gaze drifted once more toward Jeong Yeon-shin. That same strange, appraising look.

"I think she was looking at me."

"Don't flatter yourself. It was clearly me."

The bickering behind him grated on his nerves.

What difference did it make which of them a renowned Zhongnan elder happened to glance at? None of them would be taken as her disciple.

Jeong Yeon-shin turned and walked away. He made straight for the training ground, deep in thought.

'There's nowhere left to go with the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique.'

So what should he train next?

Having mastered one martial art and then standing before the sheer momentum of the Zhongnan Sect masters seemed to have shifted something in him.

His hunger for achievement was beginning to outweigh his reluctance.

'Swordsmanship... Should I finally pick up the sword?'

What would it feel like to taste the realm of true mastery through proper swordsmanship?

Jeong Yeon-shin walked toward the training ground, already pulling apart the Jeong Family Divine Sword forms he had watched as a child, examining each piece. His steps were light.

* * *

While the rest of the estate buzzed with commotion, the training ground was perfectly still.

A boy stood in a middle-guard stance, sword raised to his torso. His breathing was calm.

A single inhalation, deepened by the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique, filled his chest like clouds gathering in an open sky.

A single exhalation swept his mind clean, scattering stray thoughts the way rain washes dust from the earth.

Jeong Yeon-shin entered a state of no-self. One breath, and he was fully immersed in the sword.

Jeong Family Divine Sword. Splendor Overflowing Qi Feather Style.

The blade moved.

The Jeong Family Divine Sword comprised three sword intents and twenty-one techniques. Its character was solemn.

It drew from the Zhongnan Sword Arts at a shallow level, imbued with the particular sensibility of the Hyeonmun Jeong Sect of Taoism.

Had it been the art of the main sect, it would have been both majestic and profound. The Jeong Family Divine Sword was majestic alone.

Something was off. It felt like a secular swordsman trying to imitate a Taoist priest.

Jeong Yeon-shin sensed it instinctively, before the thought had fully formed.

What does a secular swordsman descended from a Taoist lineage actually pursue? Wealth and fame.

All he needs is to cut down whoever stands in his way with a single stroke.

And what does a swordsman who lacks depth require? Speed. The blade must be swift.

Whoosh!

The sword's character changed. The heavy, sweeping arc of the blade grew lighter.

Energy surged through every meridian in his body, following a new intent.

The genuine energy of the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique churned with sudden intensity, contracting every muscle.

Swift sword techniques wove themselves into the existing forms, and a new path opened naturally before him.

Swish!

Birds scattered from the trees, startled by the iron sword tearing through the night air.

That strike existed nowhere in the Jeong Family Divine Sword. It was something entirely new.

"Haa."

A long breath escaped him, carrying with it a wave of deep satisfaction.

Jeong Yeon-shin looked down at his hand on the sword hilt.

When he had begun the form, he had gripped with all five fingers according to the Divine Sword's method. Now the pressure on his thumb and index finger had eased, just slightly.

So that was how it worked.

"This is swiftness."

The boy who had grasped the swift sword technique on his own and forged a new move from nothing was thrumming with excitement.

It was nothing like the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique, that internal cultivation built through patient labor, sewn together stitch by painstaking stitch.

A single thought, born from seeing the Zhongnan Sect masters, and he had created something new to play with.

"It's fun. It really is."

Jeong Yeon-shin ran through the new swift sword move several more times, then raised his head.

The sky was pitch-black, as though draped in ink-colored silk.

Neither stars nor moon were visible. Clouds had swallowed the entire world.

'I should sleep if I want to be up for dawn practice.'

A satisfied smile on his face, he headed straight for his bedroom.

His body, tempered by the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique, had not shed a single drop of sweat.

Seeing no need to wash, the boy lay down and fell asleep at once.

* * *

Jeong Yeon-shin rose early, finished his morning practice, and went straight to the breakfast hall.

Normally, he had a servant bring food to his room so he could eat alone. Today was different.

The clan leader would certainly share a meal with the Zhongnan Sect masters, whose length of stay remained unknown.

'A little shamelessness never killed anyone.'

Just being near their righteous energy was enough to spark new insight.

The boy who had tasted real achievement the night before showed up to the family breakfast without an ounce of hesitation.

He had no interest in the stunned looks the other family members shot his way.

Jeong Yeon-shin fixed his gaze on the Zhongnan Sect figures seated at the head of the table.

"Ahem. Since you're already here, sit down."

Clan Leader Jeong Dae-myeong cleared his throat as he spoke.

Scolding his own son in front of distinguished martial artists would have been a poor look. He could hardly turn the boy away.

That was precisely what Jeong Yeon-shin had counted on.

The meal began.

The boy stole glances at the Zhongnan Sect members, Yeo Il-sin foremost among them, trying not to be obvious about it.

He wanted to know what kind of cultivation produced the effortless composure they radiated.

A person's energy takes on different qualities depending on their inner state. It fascinated him that these three carried the pristine energy of Mount Zhongnan as naturally as they breathed.

Then his eyes met Yeo Il-sin's. She smiled, faintly.

"The clan leader's youngest son has remarkably clear eyes. He seems quite intelligent."

"Hmm...! My deepest apologies. My third son's cultivation is severely lacking..."

Jeong Dae-myeong flinched, leaning back slightly, and shot Jeong Yeon-shin a sharp glare.

The boy lowered his head and picked up a few vegetables, chewing quietly.

Strangely, Yeo Il-sin's subtle smile unsettled him more than Jeong Dae-myeong's open scowl.

Something about her was different.

'Clarity alone doesn't account for this. Her energy is dense. Is this what they mean by the ultimate level of inner cultivation?'

He was witnessing it for the first time: genuine energy condensed so thoroughly within the body that it radiated outward on its own.

A presence so powerful it could be felt without being seen.

In that moment, every assumption he held about the upper limits of internal energy shattered.

'The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique isn't a dead end. It can go further.'

The vegetables stopped halfway to his mouth.

He had caught a glimpse of something. The faint outline of a new martial art.

It struck him now: everything he had accomplished with the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique up to this point amounted to tempering his own body.

He had been too blind to realize there was more beyond it.

No one had told him. Amid all the indifference and quiet hostility, not a single person had thought to say a word.

'The body is a vessel. To possess energy like hers, I need to cultivate energy itself.'

What he had done so far was not wrong.

By pouring everything into physical training alone, he had laid a complete foundation, a body through which genuine energy could circulate freely.

First essence, then energy. Polish the vessel first. Then fill it with water.

All that remained was to pour it in.

Jeong Yeon-shin had awakened to the most fundamental, and most absolute, of principles.

The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique could evolve further.

It would transform once more and become something new entirely.

'This time, it will be a true internal energy cultivation method.'

He hummed to himself, inwardly.

Inspirations struck his mind like bolts of lightning, one after another.

Countless methods of drawing the energy between heaven and earth into the body through breathing techniques tangled together, then began to coalesce into a framework.

'What should I call the next stage of the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique?'

For just an instant, the boy's eyes flashed sky-blue. The only person who witnessed it was Yeo Il-sin of the Zhongnan Sect.

Doubt bloomed across her uniquely beautiful face.

* * *

"The energy here is better than I expected."

"Indeed. If the land's energy is this rich, the Tyrant Sword Sect will certainly covet it."

The speakers were two Zhongnan Sect disciples who had accompanied the elders as attendants.

All three martial artists, Yeo Il-sin included, sat drinking tea in the guest quarters the Jeong family's clan leader had prepared for them.

The room was tastefully arranged, the subtle fragrance of tea lending an air of refinement, as though the hosts had taken pains to honor disciples of the Nine Sects.

"Their arrival is inevitable. The counterattack plan is sound, but... the sacrifice this family will bear still weighs on me."

Yeo Il-sin spoke.

"We keep our senses open at all times."

"Can't we simply step outside and cut them down before they breach the wall?"

Confidence shone on the faces of the two disciples, both well past thirty.

Yeo Il-sin said nothing further, feeling the fighting spirit that poured from them.

They were elites of the Zhongnan Sect, one of the Nine Sects of the world.

This was not something that could be dismissed as arrogance.

She changed the subject.

"What do you think of the child who will be taken as a disciple?"

"I'm not sure what to make of him."

"Neither am I..."

Both gave lukewarm responses.

When you have lived for over two hundred years, you can gauge a person's character and potential from a first meeting alone, reading their bearing and their words. In Yeo Il-sin's estimation, there was nothing remarkable about the Jeong family's second son.

"The third son, on the other hand. There was resolve in his eyes."

"I noticed the same."

"Even at a glance, he is no ordinary vessel. He may well become the sword that carries this family's name in the future. Since a named disciple of the main sect cannot inherit the family, perhaps the clan leader is deliberately sending the second son instead."

Yeo Il-sin's words were framed as speculation, but her tone left no room for doubt. Surprise flickered across the two disciples' faces.

"That highly?"

"Then should we bring the third son to the main sect after all...?"

"That would violate the established rules. The sect has already designated a named disciple. Even if we reversed the decision now, the clan leader would never agree to it, and the ceremonies of the Great Zhongnan are not to be taken lightly."

The disciples bowed their heads at the elder's quiet rebuke, chastened by the realization that they had spoken carelessly.

Leaving them to their reflections, Yeo Il-sin tapped the table with her long fingers.

'He is a strange child, that one.'

Time and again, he had shown a peculiar quality.

It was not the trait the noble clans called "being beloved by nature."

If it were, the signs that only Yeo Il-sin could perceive would have enveloped the boy like a mantle.

This was something else entirely.

'That child seemed to stand apart from heaven and earth. Singular. Distinct.'

Supreme masters of internal energy, those at her level, commune with the heavens themselves.

The genuine energy that pierced the Baihui acupoint at the crown of the skull was a truly mysterious thing, often granting the upper dantian something akin to foresight.

For Yeo Il-sin to feel this degree of unease, the child had to be extraordinary.

But this was not the time to concern herself with such things.

"The Tyrant Sword Sect is a formidable enemy. Do not let your guard down. Sharpen your sword energy."

* * *

After breakfast, the steward came to see Jeong Yeon-shin. He could already guess what this was about.

The man's polite face wore an uncomfortable expression as he regarded the boy.

"Steward."

"Third Young Master."

"I've been wondering lately whether I've done something wrong. I keep turning it over in my mind."

"Third Young Master."

"My mother's energy was exhausted when I was born. Is that why I'm not even permitted to show my face before the clan's honored guests?"

"...It is the clan leader's will."

The steward answered. Seeing the strain written plainly across his face, Jeong Yeon-shin let the matter drop.

The steward was a good man.

He was the hands and feet of the clan, overseeing every affair large and small according to Jeong Dae-myeong's orders, regardless of his own feelings on the matter.

At times like these, Jeong Yeon-shin found himself missing his maternal family, the ones he had never met.

But the Jeong family never sent him to them.

His mother's family had ties to a very powerful force.

After his birth mother closed her eyes, they never once came seeking their grandson. Yet the family members who treated Jeong Yeon-shin so coldly remained perpetually wary and ill at ease because of that connection.

"I'm sorry."

The boy said. A fleeting look of pity crossed the steward's face.

"...I've heard the training ground is short on materials, Young Master. I will have new ones procured."

"Thank you. I'll be waiting."

As Jeong Yeon-shin turned to leave, he reproached himself.

Thanks to gaining enlightenment whenever
